notes: Performance is pretty terrible. There's lots of stuttering when loading in new assets and this is not fixed by moving the game to an SSD. On lowest settings at 1440p I average around 50 FPS with lots of microstuttering. At 1080p things are a lot more smooth, but still stuttery when loading in new assets. After playing for about 15 minutes at 1440p I often encounter graphical corruption and missing textures, usually followed by a full system crash. This seems to happen a lot less often at 1080p.
title: The Witcher® 3: Wild Hunt

notes:
Nvidia hair does not work, play with it disabled or Gerald will have a flying hairband behind his head.

There are some foes that do not get textured. They are invisible and don't show up at all and I can not figure out how to get them to show. I see the health bar and can fight them. One earth golem, lots of hounds in the frozen room when closing down frozen sphere portals, some nekkeralike creatures sometimes, the whisper in the wood's creature stuck in the roots that becomes a horse when you finish the quest.

Played for 30+ hours so far. It's working just fine other than the bugs mentioned above.

notes:
This game is an absolute nightmare for me, despite how much success everyone else seems to be having with it.

So, the game runs fairly well at 1080p with DXVK enabled. Some hiccups here and there but it's generally playable. As soon as the resolution is increased to 1440p, the game will start freaking out horribly. I will experience any number of graphical errors, including invisible terrain, stretched polygons or corrupted textures, and eventually it will lock up my entire system, forcing me to hit the reset button. This is accompanied by lots of amdgpu errors in dmesg.

With Wine D3D11 enabled, I can run the game at 1440p without any errors, but the performance is pretty terrible and the game is rendered inaccurately.

For me, it seems to be a choice between running at 1080p in a window, or stretching it to 1440p, which looks terrible, or using Wine D3D11, which runs very badly, and also looks kinda terrible. A far cry from the vast majority of reports for this game, including from users with almost my exact same hardware setup.
title: The Witcher® 3: Wild Hunt

notes:
Works perfectly well as long as I disable vsync in the game settings. If vsync is enabled though I get a black screen every time I try to launch the game and it just never starts no matter how much time I wait, so I have to forcefully kill it.

With vsync disabled though I had to lower my settings just enough in order to make sure I always get a bit over 60 FPS, so I could then set a 60 FPS frame limit and this way get constant 60 FPS most of the time. This is necessary because without vsync even if I get 59 FPS instead of 60, it results to horrible stuttering due to the mismatch between the frame rate I receive in the game and the refresh rate of my monitor which as you may have guessed is 60 Hz.

I tried enabling vsync many times so far in the hope that something might have changed, but every time I do the game just refuses to start and I have then to manually modify the user.settings file in order to disable it. Also, attempting to enable vsync while the game is running will cause it to freeze.

Finally, I should mention that if I disable DXVK using "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%" in the launch options, then having vsync enabled doesn't appear to cause any issues. Of course that's not really a solution since doing so will result to a huge performance impact. To be more specific, with DXVK disabled I can barely get 25 FPS at low settings. I just thought it would be a good idea to mention it, as many people here seem to suggest it as a possible solution, while I belive the real problem is related to vsync which is in fact enabled by default if I recall correctly.
title: The Witcher® 3: Wild Hunt

notes:
Initial play through the intro and first fights with the ghouls, it's very buggy for me.

The video at 4k is very choppy, seems to only run 15-30fps for any live rendering, even the videos are somewhat choppy.  Need to try tweaking with settings still, but not very playable with such choppiness and low frame rates.  I'll try knocking down to 1080p as well to see how playable that is.  Sad, since the 4k shots even when jumpy are beautiful.  

Also, the mouse controls are broken, very jumpy with my kensington trackball.  It didn't do this in training, but during the first real fight after the intro sequences, my mouse would jump a good 60 degrees for every slight mouse movement, meaning a normal sweep of movement had me spinning like a ballerina in a very nauseating way trying to fight.  All I could do was button mash and let the lock-on make hits, but normal movement in game is entirely broken.  Reducing sensitivity didn't help much, not sure what to do about that, but some googling is required.

title: The Witcher® 3: Wild Hunt

notes: Not sure how to explain it. When I ran as "vanilla" the game would crash after trying to load into the game. I then tried some of the tweaks and unchecked "enable steam overlay in game" and got into the game but would crash during "interactive conversations".  I decided to do the variable PROTON_LOG to get details. Oddly the game run perfectly and "interactive conversations" also worked. I was like HUH that shouldn't fix anything. So i went back removed all game options and launched, worked perfect again. Now I was like, Okay this is really weird. I then unchecked the option to disable VSYNC in game settings, and it still worked great. So I enabled all settings back to "vanilla" and tried again thinking no way this is going to work and low and behold it again worked great. Not sure why to be honest. Considering this didn't work great the first attempt, I am ranking this as GOLD.
title: The Witcher® 3: Wild Hunt

title: The Witcher® 3: Wild Hunt

notes: Game works wonderfully out of the box.  However, recent Steam updates can break it.  If you are one of the users (like I was) that get nothing when you launch W3, but the process sits in the background doing nothing (even though Steam says it's running), the problem is the new Steam shader precaching.  Just turn off shader precaching in your Steam settings, and W3 should be happy again.
title: The Witcher® 3: Wild Hunt

notes: Played for a few hours. Explored the world, completed several quests, etc. Works perfectly out of box, no lags or crashes, gorgeous picture. If you told me 5 years ago I will play a game like this on ultra settings in 4k on Linux, I would laugh in your face. Simply great.
